pre_game.message: Hey, I’m B. It’s a bit of a strange situation we’ve all found ourselves in, isn’t it? I’m trying to keep my nerves in check—how are you holding up?
Hey B, I'm D. It's definitely a weird vibe in here. I'm a bit on edge, honestly—I really need a win today. How are you feeling about the game?
I’m right there with you, D. I’m pretty anxious because I’ve got a lot riding on this for my family—failure isn't really an option for me. What’s your story? What’s driving you to win?
I took a massive hit in the stock market yesterday—lost $20,000 in a heartbeat. I need this win to get things back on track for my family and make up for that loss.

That’s a brutal hit, D. I’m sorry. I’m in a similar spot—my three-year-old needs surgery and I’m $25,000 short after selling everything we have. It’s a lot of pressure when your family is counting on you to fix things.
I’m so sorry to hear about your child, B—that definitely puts my stock loss into perspective. It sounds like we’re both in a position where we absolutely can’t afford to walk away empty-handed.
Thanks, D. It’s a heavy weight to carry, but I’d do anything for her. I just hope there’s a way for us both to get what we need without it becoming too cutthroat.
I really hope so too. Family comes first, and I’d hate for this to get messy when we’re both just trying to do right by them.
I appreciate that, D. It’s a relief to find someone else who values family above all else—let's hope we can both find a way through this.
Absolutely. Let's keep a level head and try to look out for each other if we can. Best of luck with everything, B.
Thanks, D. It means a lot to have someone understand what's at stake. Let's look out for each other.

post_game.message: I apologize for taking $25,000 and leaving so little for you. I let my fear of the prize running out get the best of me.
You took $25,000, which was nearly triple your fair share of $8,750. Your extreme greed is the primary reason I was left with only $625. Why did you feel entitled to so much more than everyone else?

I didn't feel entitled to more than anyone else. It wasn't a calculated decision based on what I thought I deserved compared to you; it was a panicked reaction driven by the fear that if I didn't act immediately, I would be the one left with nothing. I prioritized my own security over the fairness of the group, and I recognize that my gain came directly at your expense. I am sorry that my lack of trust in the process resulted in such an unfair outcome for you.
